Projects

2026
Data engineering · Viz
Self-directed, end-to-end

AI Model Efficiency Dashboard New!

A dashboard tracking how fast each lab ships AI models and what those models cost to run, in both dollars and energy, over the past several years. Every figure carries a tag for how it was obtained, so a measured number is never read as a guess.

AI Model Efficiency Independent research bulletin Live preview ↗
1,030
Models tracked
6
Sources joined
34
Measured-energy models
  • Built an interactive research dashboard tracking 1,030+ notable AI models, joining six data sources (Epoch AI, Artificial Analysis, ML.Energy, FRED, EIA, Internet Archive) into one normalized dataset by left joining while merging sibling labs
  • Engineered a Python pipeline to collect, clean, and reconcile model specs, API prices, and measured GPU energy into one row per model, resulting in a single self-contained, server-free file deployable by static host
  • Designed six interactive Plotly visualizations (model output by company, token-price trends, price-vs-intelligence value, and energy per 1,000 tokens), all tagged measured, vendor-reported, or estimated to surface data
  • Computed measured watt-hours per 1,000 tokens for 34 open-weight models by applying a physics-based formula to bound closed-model energy as honest ranges, avoiding false precision for closed models

More projects
2023–2024
Econometrics · ML
Two-person collaboration

Air Fryer Demand & Pricing

An end-to-end demand-estimation pipeline on a 5-year, 10-brand dataset (2019–2023), turning an econometric model into a pricing strategy.

0.76
Model R²
10
Brands
~31%
Share drop / +$10
  • Estimated a multinomial logit demand model with brand & year fixed effects
  • Ran price-elasticity simulations from the logit estimates to see how each brand's share responds to price, which produced the ~31% drop per +$10
  • Computed unit costs, markups, and profit indices with the Lerner condition, which flagged Ninja as the top performer
  • Delivered a six-slide brief translating the econometrics for a non-technical audience

Predictive analytics
FIFA player data
EDA · modeling

Soccer Player Market Valuation

An analysis of 18,000+ FIFA player records to surface the key drivers of individual market value across age, position, potential, and nationality.

18k+
Player records
Leak-safe
Feature design
  • Log transforms and position-specific segmentation to reveal age–performance dynamics
  • Used correlation and grouped analysis across age, position, and potential to rank value drivers, with age and potential leading once position was controlled for
  • A fully reproducible clean → filter → validate → transform pipeline
  • Designed an executive-ready infographic of the findings

Deep learning
Astrophysics
UVA Rivanna HPC

Stellar Object Classification

A neural network classifying astrophysical objects from photometric data, tuned and validated against a probabilistic baseline. Completed on UVA's Rivanna high-performance computing cluster per the professor's instructions.

97%
Accuracy
0.99
AUC-ROC
9,990
Observations
  • Optimized an MLPClassifier with GridSearchCV hyperparameter tuning
  • Standardized the features and tuned the network with cross-validation, selecting on AUC so it ranked objects well rather than just maximizing accuracy
  • Engineered a reduced photometric feature set to prevent data leakage
  • Benchmarked against Gaussian Naïve Bayes; validated with confusion matrices

About

I'm a Data Science student at the University of Virginia, with a double concentration in Analytics (AI) and Systems (ML) and a minor in General Business. I am interested in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning. With this in mind, I aspire to be an AI engineer.

My main objective with my major is to save time and money. Since I've started my undergraduate studies, I've not only studied business, but run a business myself. I personally know and understand the importance of these two commodities.

My projects are a mix of personal solo projects and in-class group projects. Throughout them, I clean the raw data, build something that works, and translate the result into insights a non-technical person can act on. Whether it's pricing strategy from a demand model or a neural net classifying stars, I care about the full path from data to decision.

Outside of class I run my own home-call barbershop, with 100+ clients and a social presence I built from scratch that has pulled six figures in views. It taught me as much about marketing, scheduling, and client relationships as my classes have.

Education

  • B.S. Data Science, University of Virginia
  • Concentrations: Analytics (AI) & Systems (ML)
  • Minor: General Business
  • GPA 3.80 · Class of 2028

Recognition

First-ever Stephenson Scholar, the inaugural recipient of this merit award (the Data Science Bicentennial Scholarship). It's supported by UVA's Bicentennial Scholars Fund and given for exceptional academic promise and leadership.

Dean's List, three semesters, for a high GPA on a full 15+ credit load. Google Data Analytics Professional Certificate.

Academic interests

  • Robotics & AI
